skill.name: convex
skill.description: Routes Convex development requests to focused companion skills for functions, schema design, realtime updates, storage, agents, security, and best practices.
skill.summary: Use the right Convex companion skill for each backend development task.
skill.author: Convex

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution findings are false positives caused by Markdown slash-command names, not executable shell or Ruby backtick code. The network findings are documentation links only, and the weak-cryptography alert is a false positive on metadata text at SKILL.md line 4.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Slash Commands Are Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged lines 18, 19, 20, 21, 27, 28, 34, 35, 36, 42, 43, 49, 54, 55, and 56 as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines are Markdown table entries and numbered-list references to companion skill slash commands. No executable command runner or shell invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens appear inside Markdown documentation tables and quick-start text. There is no code block, interpreter directive, or API that would execute these strings.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation URLs Only
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged hardcoded URLs at lines 61 and 62. These are Convex documentation links and do not perform network requests, transmit data, or collect credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs are plain Markdown documentation references. No fetch, curl, webhook, script include, or runtime network behavior is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Metadata Text Mistaken for Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 4 as weak cryptography. The line is the skill description metadata and contains no cryptographic algorithm, library call, key handling, or security-sensitive operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 4 is descriptive frontmatter text for the marketplace. I found no evidence of cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm use in the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: Convex projects often need different guidance for schema, functions, realtime data, storage, agents, and security. This skill routes each request to the most relevant Convex companion skill and documentation path.
actual_capabilities: Maps Convex tasks to focused companion skills for functions, schema, realtime, storage, migrations, agents, cron jobs, and components.
Provides a quick-start path for common Convex development work.
Identifies security-focused companion skills for checklist reviews and deeper audits.
Links users to primary Convex documentation and an LLM-optimized documentation file.
Organizes Convex guidance into core development, data, storage, advanced patterns, security, and best practices.
limitations: It is an index skill and does not contain detailed implementation guidance itself.
It depends on companion Convex skills being installed and available in the user environment.
Documentation links require network access when users want to open external Convex references.
It does not run automated tests, migrations, deployments, or security scanners.

use_cases.title: Start a Convex Feature
use_cases.description: Select the right companion skill before writing queries, mutations, actions, validators, or subscriptions.

use_cases.title: Plan Backend Architecture
use_cases.description: Route schema, storage, realtime, cron, and component questions to the matching Convex guidance area.

use_cases.title: Review Convex Security
use_cases.description: Find the security checklist or deeper audit skill when reviewing permissions, endpoints, and data access patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Convex Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose the right Convex companion skill for my task: [describe the feature or problem].

prompt_templates.title: Start a New Data Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Route me to the best Convex guidance for designing tables, validators, indexes, and related backend functions.

prompt_templates.title: Build Realtime Behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: I need realtime updates in a Convex app. Identify the companion skills I should use and the order to use them.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Convex System
prompt_templates.prompt: Route a complete Convex review covering functions, HTTP actions, storage, agents, scheduled jobs, schema, and security risks.

output_examples.input: I need to write a mutation and validate new document fields.
output_examples.output: Use the functions guidance for mutation structure, then use the schema validator guidance for table fields and validators.

output_examples.input: I am adding uploads and public file serving to a Convex app.
output_examples.output: Use the file storage guidance first, then review security guidance for access rules and public serving decisions.

output_examples.input: I want a scheduled cleanup job that updates old records.
output_examples.output: Use the cron jobs guidance for scheduling, the functions guidance for the update logic, and migrations guidance if existing data needs backfill work.

best_practices: Start with best practices when the request spans multiple Convex areas.
Use schema and validator guidance before writing functions that depend on new data shapes.
Route security-sensitive work to the checklist or audit guidance before implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not treat this index as a replacement for detailed Convex implementation guidance.
Do not skip security guidance for HTTP actions, file access, agents, or public endpoints.
Do not use migration guidance only after production data has already diverged from the schema.

faq.question: What does this Convex skill do?
faq.answer: It routes Convex development questions to focused companion skills for implementation, architecture, and security topics.

faq.question: Does it write Convex functions directly?
faq.answer: No. It points you to the functions companion skill for queries, mutations, and actions.

faq.question: Can it help with database schema design?
faq.answer: Yes. It directs schema and validation work to the schema validator companion skill.

faq.question: Does it cover realtime subscriptions?
faq.answer: Yes. It identifies the realtime companion skill for reactive subscriptions and live application behavior.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use?
faq.answer: The reviewed file contains documentation and routing text only. No executable code, shell execution, or data exfiltration was found.

faq.question: When should I use the security audit guidance?
faq.answer: Use it for deeper reviews of permissions, HTTP endpoints, storage access, agents, and data exposure risks.
